personas.name: Aion Engine
personas.role: Dispatching activities and managing workflow lifecycle

stories.text: As the Aion Engine, I want to dispatch an activity to an available worker via a liminal conversation so that I can replace gRPC infrastructure with the same process-level primitives I already use for workflow supervision.
As the Aion Engine, I want immediate notification when a worker crashes during activity execution so that I can re-dispatch the activity to another worker without waiting for a heartbeat timeout.

As the Aion Engine, I want to deliver signals to running workflows through typed liminal channels so that signal delivery is validated and integrated with the same channel infrastructure used for activity dispatch.
As the Aion Engine, I want to publish workflow history events to a durable channel so that consumers can stream history without coupling to the event store API.
As the Aion Engine, I want all channel operations in the integration surface to be capturable by the recorder so that workflow recovery replays dispatch and signal operations deterministically.
As an Aion Worker, I want to register for activity dispatch by subscribing to a task queue channel so that I receive work through the same channel primitives the bus uses for all consumers.
As an Aion Worker, I want to declare my processing capacity at subscription time so that the routing function respects my concurrency limits when selecting me for dispatch.
As an Aion Worker, I want to report activity completion, failure, or heartbeat through the dispatch conversation handle so that the engine tracks my progress through a single communication path.
As a Workflow Author, I want activity dispatch to be transparent so that my workflow code uses the same schedule_activity API regardless of whether dispatch routes through liminal or a local call.
As a Workflow Author, I want typed activity inputs and outputs validated at dispatch time so that schema mismatches are caught before the worker begins execution.
As a Workflow Author, I want signals delivered reliably to my workflow so that I can react to external events with confidence that validated signals arrive in order.
As an Operator, I want to scale the worker pool by starting or stopping worker processes so that I can adjust capacity without configuration changes or rebalancing downtime.
As an Operator, I want to stream workflow history from a channel so that I can build monitoring dashboards and debugging tools without querying the event store directly.
As an Operator, I want worker pool health observable through channel subscription metrics so that I can detect capacity shortfalls before they stall workflow execution.
